1. upa-√1. dā P. -dadāti, to give in addition, add;
to give, grant, offer,  RV. vi, 28, 2 ;  AV. iv, 21, 2 ; xix, 34, 8 ;  R. ;
to take upon one's self:
Pass. (irr. p. -dadyamāna) to be offered or granted (as protection),  RV. vi, 49, 13.

results.rendered_html:
upadā 3 U.
1 To give in addition.
2 To offer, give, grant (in general).
3 To add.
4 (1 A.) (Ved.) (a) To take upon oneself. (b) To erect, raise, support.

results.rendered_html:
upadā a. Ved. Giving a present.
— dā (cf.  P. III. 3. 106 )
1 A present, an offering to a king or a great man, a Nazarānā; upadā viviśuḥ śaśvannotsekāḥ kosaleśvaraṃ  R. 4. 70 , 5. 41 , 7. 30 ;  P. V. 1. 47. 
2 A bribe.
